subject: Accounting
title: Reading Retained Earnings
description: A 60-second lesson on Reading Retained Earnings.

hook.text: A company has been profitable for twenty years but retained earnings are near zero. What happened?

buildup.text: Retained earnings accumulate every dollar of profit kept in the business since day one.

discovery.text: They shrink when dividends are paid or when the company posts losses.

twist.text: Some companies deliberately keep retained earnings low by returning cash to shareholders.

climax.text: Negative retained earnings signal a company has lost more money over its life than it's earned.

punchline.text: Retained earnings are the company's lifetime report card.
